reviews.review_msg: Steve was an amazing lecturer and provided content that was very much helpful to my real life. He is a tough grader in that he often marks based on negative marks (subtracts for what you get wrong instead of adds for what you get right), but he provides you practice quizzes and in-class quizzes (not for marks) that are extremely similar to the exam
avoid him at all costs if you can even if u have to wait until next semester for a new prof, it'll be absolutely worth it.

The concepts were tough, and he wasn't the best at explaining . Lecture slides were formatted poorly and showed up all scrambled up when you tried to download them. Be ready to do a quiz like every class, plus 2 mid terms and a final. The course was way too test heavy. if you do bad on quizzes or exams,  nothing else brings your grade up.

Amateur lecture presentations looked like it was done be a kid in high school. Grading for exams was all over the place no consistency. Doesn't seem like he cares about teaching or wants to see his students succeed. Doesn't offer help outside of office hours. If you take this class get ready to learn everything on your own
He  is very humorous
Hey, at least he is fair. I dont know why he has this low of a rating, at least he doesnt go around accusing people of cheating like Layla Cameron. Solid prof overall, you just need to study!

When I read the reviews before taking the prof I was scared cause everyone said he was really horrible but when I took the class, he's not bad at all. he's funny at points, he makes dad jokes haha but of you just stick to what he asks of you then you are good to go. follow his instructions and you will get a good grade. just a bit grumpy

Honestly not as bad as I expected it to be. Be ready for pop quizes. But not very difficult if you keep up with class materials. I would reccomend attend every class and especially pay attention to in class examples/ questions. He does tend to ramble but explains the questions well. If you don't understand, there's always AI.

DO NOT TAKE THIS CLASS!!!!!!!!!! Worst prof i've ever taken, has a huge ego but not confident and can barely maintain any eye contact. avoids accountability on EVERYTHINGGG. He forces you to buy the book first class and never used it once. He tends to ramble alot but there is no content to his yapping so its just 4 hours of ur day wasted. 0/10 prof
His classes reminded me of life in prison
A living legend. If you can't handle the heat, get out of the kitchen. Steve is an example of Accounting upper division courses at Beedie.

Follow instructions, Do as he says, and you will be fine. He is scary yes, but tbh I think it is all part of a persona he likes to play during class to frighten students. Don't try to play dumb on him, he is NOT scared of giving zeros. 

Fun fact: he is a Taylor swift and hello kitty fan (pay attention to lectures and you'll notice lol)

Honestly, I can tell you that it's pretty rough with this professor. He's just mean and doesn't explain the assignments or content well at all. his explanations can be super confusing

I wouldn't say I hate the guy, but he's just not a great teacher. If you take this course with him be ready to fail. I recommend looking for another professor.

Does not teach anything about accounting and already assumes you know what to do. Rambles a lot in class about Taylor Swift, Elon Musk, and how America is so great and China being inferior. You will learn more about BUS251 in youtube, just look for "Tony Bell" channel and you will have everything you need.

Just RUN
He is not a good speaker and have no passion in teaching, always degrading students tell you you are not smart enough to pass the course.
worst teacher in FIC.. i suggest dropping his course.

If you are an incoming fic stuents, wanting to take 216 DO NOT take this prof. He will make sure your life in this course misrable with his stuttering speech, and very tough grading in the presentation
Did that one review said he is easy after failing 2 midterms lol

Not a good speaker. I don't know why he is teaching, I sense no passion. More like just a job for him. I learned by looking at worked out solutions, not by listening from him. I'm nearly done my degree and have had my fair share of good and bad profs. This prof is in my bad profs list in terms of quality and attitude. Worst of all is the attitude.

Steve regularly questions the capability of his students and takes no responsibility for his student's failures. He is blunt, rude, and unaccomodating, overcomplicating his lecture material to the point where self-teaching is necessary. Truly unfortunate that Business Minor students are stuck with only one professor for this course.
boring lectures, not engaging at all. the curve saved me
No no no guys. Just run...no no...

He is very unorganized and it is very boring. He seems to make up a lot of content on the spot and rambles about personal matters more than lecture content. He is also very unempathic and does not care to answer students questions or support their learning. DO NOT TAKE HIS CLASS.
Never met this instructor made the midterm exam like this hard.....
GOOD LUCK!!!! Horrible.....
Rambles ALOT, lectures are super boring!

Very boring, non stop stuttering in lectures, barley knows anything about the course he is teaching. Unclear assignments, unclear deadlines, changes assignment instructions the day before they are due, changed course grading scheme multiple times throughout the semester. Assignments aren't too hard but not a good overall experience.
Run for your life! ! ! DON'T TAKE IT! ! Just leave him without any reason. Otherwise, you will fail from our respectful "Steve-o & Gibco" company owner :)
It was perfect, down to the nearest minute detail. Very good prof, amazing notes and challenging homework and samples. This course is not for the weak tho.

The prof is not for softies and encouragement-seeking weak-minded individuals. Yes, the course is super hard and yes, the prof makes it harder. However, I still loved every second of the 13 4-hours lectures because of the based jokes from this guy. Your grade and your ability to get along the prof will determine if BBA is really for you.
